content:
Google open-sourced a tool that connects AI agents directly to 30+ databases. One config file. No boilerplate.
The reasoning layer works. The tool calling works. Then you need the agent to actually query your data, and suddenly you're writing connection pooling, handling auth, managing schema discovery, and building security controls before the agent can touch a single row.
Every team rebuilds this from scratch.
Google built it once and open-sourced it.
MCP Toolbox for Databases connects Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI directly to your databases out of the box. Add it to your MCP config, point it at your database, and your AI agent can query your data in plain English, explore schemas, and generate database-aware code - without leaving your IDE.
For production agents it goes further:
→ Structured queries with predefined logic so agents can't go rogue 
→ Semantic search and NL2SQL built in 
→ IAM authentication and restricted access controls out of the box 
→ Connection pooling handled automatically 
→ Full observability via OpenTelemetry - metrics and traces without configuration
Supported out of the box: 
→ Google Cloud: AlloyDB, BigQuery, Cloud SQL, Spanner, Firestore, Dataplex 
→ Everything else: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, MongoDB, Redis, Snowflake, Elasticsearch, Neo4j, ClickHouse and more
The detail most people will miss => Google started building this before MCP existed. When MCP became the standard they renamed it and open-sourced it. This isn't a repo built to chase a trend. It's production infrastructure that predates one.
